message:
test(fuzz): add fuzz_store_hll target for fr_store HyperLogLog surface

Adds a dedicated libFuzzer harness exercising fr_store's HyperLogLog
path:

- fuzz/Cargo.toml registers the new `fuzz_store_hll` binary with the
  standard per-target settings (test = false, doc = false, bench =
  false, path = fuzz_targets/fuzz_store_hll.rs) to match the existing
  23+ fr_store / fr_protocol / fr_runtime fuzz targets.

- fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_store_hll.rs (~440 lines) defines an
  Arbitrary-derived FuzzInput + HllOp enum that drives the Store API
  via PFADD/PFCOUNT/PFMERGE-equivalent operations with bounded
  parameters:
    * MAX_INPUT_LEN = 4_096 bytes per invocation — clips
      Unstructured-provided inputs so libFuzzer exec/s stays under
      the anti-starvation threshold.
    * MAX_OPS = 64 operations per test case.
    * MAX_BATCH_ITEMS = 12 elements per PFADD batch.
    * MAX_BLOB_LEN = 32 bytes per individual HLL element.
    * HLL_SLOT_COUNT = 4 distinct fuzz HLL keys (fuzz:hll:0..3) plus
      named fixtures for wrong-type, invalid-binary, roundtrip,
      temp-all-sources / temp-unique-sources merge destinations, and
      a deliberately-missing key path.

  The harness drives a real `Store` instance (no mocks), exercises
  PFADD / PFCOUNT / PFMERGE semantics plus wrong-type-on-HLL-op /
  corrupt-binary-payload / PFCOUNT-missing-key / PFMERGE-into-fresh-
  vs-existing-destination branches, and asserts invariants across
  each step (PFCOUNT non-negative, PFMERGE result >= max(source
  counts), wrong-type ops return StoreError::WrongType rather than
  crashing).

Closes the fr_store-HLL fuzz gap tracked on the review backlog.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -252,3 +252,10 @@ path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_resp_configured_sequences.rs"
 test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
+
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_store_hll"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_store_hll.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
